name: French Apple-Cheese Tart
recipeIngredient: Granulated sugar substitute - to equal 4 tbsp sugar or equivalent fructose
recipeInstructions:
1. Allow pie shell to defrost at room temperature. Remove it from its foil pan by carefully folding in quarters. Unfold it in a round nonstick 9-inch straight-sided cake pan. Center the pastry, then press it to fit with your fingertips, starting in the center. Use gentle pressure to stretch the pie shell up the sides of the cake pan.
2. Combine the sliced apples, raisins, cinnamon, and nutmeg; mix lightly. Spoon into the pie shell. Sprinkle with sugar substitute.
3. Combine remaining ingredients in blender or food processor and blend smooth. Pour over apple filling.
4. Bake uncovered on the bottom rack of a preheated 425°F oven for 15 minutes. Lower heat to 325°F and bake an additional 25 to 30 minutes, or until cheese topping is set.
